t: Which is greater: 47 or 74?
e: Compare the tens first. 74 has 7 tens and 47 has 4 tens. Since 7 > 4, we know 74 > 47.

t: Which symbol goes in the blank? 385 ___ 583
e: Compare hundreds first. 385 has 3 hundreds, 583 has 5 hundreds. 3 < 5, so 385 < 583.

t: Which number is the smallest: 216, 621, 162?
e: Compare hundreds: 162 has 1 hundred, 216 has 2, 621 has 6. 1 is the smallest, so 162 is the smallest number.

t: Is 500 greater than, less than, or equal to 499?
e: 500 has 5 hundreds and 499 has 4 hundreds. Since 5 > 4, we know 500 > 499.

t: Which symbol goes in the blank? 250 ___ 250
e: Both numbers are exactly the same: 250 = 250. Use the equal sign.

t: Which is greater: 892 or 829?
e: Both have 8 hundreds. Compare tens: 892 has 9 tens, 829 has 2 tens. Since 9 > 2, 892 > 829.

t: Put in order from least to greatest: 100, 10, 1
e: From least to greatest: 1 is the smallest, then 10, then 100. Answer: 1, 10, 100.

t: Which number is the greatest: 345, 354, 435?
e: Compare hundreds first. 435 has 4 hundreds, while 345 and 354 only have 3 hundreds. 435 is the greatest.

t: Which symbol goes in the blank? 607 ___ 670
e: Both have 6 hundreds. 607 has 0 tens, 670 has 7 tens. 0 < 7, so 607 < 670.

t: Is 1,000 greater than 999?
e: 1,000 has 4 digits and 999 has 3 digits. A 4-digit number is always greater than a 3-digit number. 1,000 > 999.

t: Which number is less: 418 or 481?
e: Both have 4 hundreds. Compare tens: 418 has 1 ten, 481 has 8 tens. 1 < 8, so 418 < 481.

t: Sara has 312 stickers. Tom has 321 stickers. Who has more?
e: Both have 3 hundreds. Compare tens: 312 has 1 ten, 321 has 2 tens. 2 > 1, so Tom has more.

t: Which symbol goes in the blank? 755 ___ 575
e: Compare hundreds: 755 has 7 hundreds, 575 has 5 hundreds. 7 > 5, so 755 > 575.

t: Which is the greatest number: 199, 200, 188?
e: 200 has 2 hundreds while 199 and 188 only have 1 hundred. 200 is the greatest.

t: Is 340 greater than or less than 304?
e: Both have 3 hundreds. Compare tens: 340 has 4 tens, 304 has 0 tens. 4 > 0, so 340 > 304.

t: Which number comes just BEFORE 600?
e: The number just before 600 is 599. Count: ...598, 599, 600.

t: Which number comes just AFTER 999?
e: The number just after 999 is 1,000. This is where we go from a 3-digit number to a 4-digit number!

t: Leo picked a number between 450 and 460. Which could it be?
e: Between 450 and 460 means numbers like 451, 452... 459. 455 fits!

t: Which is less: 1,100 or 1,010?
e: Both have 1 thousand. Compare hundreds: 1,100 has 1 hundred, 1,010 has 0 hundreds. 0 < 1, so 1,010 < 1,100.

t: Which symbol goes in the blank? 900 ___ 900
e: Both numbers are 900. They are exactly equal, so we use =.
